touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Fatima Huang at SolarMedia. No answer, left voicemail introducing our AI notetaker for sales teams and mentioning productivity gains for growing sales orgs.

Follow-up email to Fatima introducing our platform as an alternative to manual note-taking and competitors like Fireflies. Highlighted rep productivity use case for mid-market sales teams. No response received.

LinkedIn message to Fatima mentioning her recent promotion to Head of Sales and asking if she's looking at tools to help her team scale. Fatima responded expressing interest in learning more about automation for her 15-person team.

questions_asked.question: What specifically does your platform automate?
questions_asked.answer: We automate call transcription, CRM updates, follow-up emails, and meeting summaries so reps can focus on selling rather than admin work.

touches.ai_summary: Email confirming Fatima's interest in a qualification call. She mentioned they're currently using Fireflies but looking for something with more automation capabilities. Agreed to 15-minute call next Tuesday.

questions_asked.question: How does your unlimited automation tier differ from basic transcription tools?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited plan includes workflow automation, custom integrations, and AI-powered insights beyond just transcription—essentially removing all manual CRM work.

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification call with Fatima. Discussed SolarMedia's current pain points with manual CRM entry and limited automation in Fireflies. Fatima is 3 months into her Head of Sales role and looking to establish credibility with quick wins. Team of 15 reps, budget approved for productivity tools.

questions_asked.question: What's your implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: We can typically get teams up and running within 2 weeks, with full automation workflows configured based on your CRM setup.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Fatima mentioned Madison Singh (IT Director) for technical review and Sebastian Iyer who handles procurement, but said she can drive the decision.

next_steps: Send case study on rep productivity gains

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Fatima diving into SolarMedia's sales process, current tech stack, and specific productivity bottlenecks. Reps spending 90+ minutes daily on CRM updates. Fireflies provides transcription but lacks automation. Fatima wants to prove ROI in her first year. Discussion around 15 seats, unlimited automation tier. She hasn't looped in exec team yet but feels confident in her authority to evaluate.

questions_asked.question: How do you calculate ROI for sales productivity tools?
questions_asked.answer: We typically see 5-7 hours saved per rep per week, which translates to roughly 15% more selling time. We can model this against your average deal size and close rate.

questions_asked.question: What does implementation look like with our Salesforce instance?
questions_asked.answer: We have native Salesforce integration—mapping fields, automating updates, and syncing meeting data takes about 3-5 business days with our team handling the heavy lifting.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to move away from Fireflies now?
questions_asked.answer: Fatima cited lack of automation and needing to show quick wins in her new role. Team growth planned for Q2 makes this a good time to standardize on better tooling.

objections: We're already paying for Fireflies—need to justify switching costs

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Fatima showing unlimited automation workflows, CRM sync, and AI-generated follow-ups. Walked through rep productivity dashboard and time-saved metrics. Fatima impressed with automation depth compared to Fireflies. Discussed pricing briefly—she mentioned budget around $12K annually. Asked about bringing in Madison Singh for technical validation.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize which fields auto-populate in Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable field mapping and you can set rules for which data points trigger CRM updates.

questions_asked.question: What's your data retention and security model?
questions_asked.answer: Enterprise-grade encryption, SOC 2 compliant, data retention configurable by admin. Happy to connect Madison with our security team for deep dive.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Fatima wants to see measurable time savings and higher CRM data quality to present to leadership as proof her team is operating more efficiently.

next_steps: Send proposal for 15 seats, unlimited tier
Coordinate technical call with Madison Singh

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Fatima. Presented $12,300 ACV for 15 seats on unlimited automation tier. Fatima comfortable with pricing and sees clear ROI. Mentioned she'll need to loop in Sebastian Iyer from procurement for contract review but expects smooth approval. Noted she hasn't briefed her VP or CEO yet but plans to after technical validation.

questions_asked.question: Is this pricing locked for multi-year or just annual?
questions_asked.answer: This is annual pricing—we can discuss multi-year discounts if you want to commit longer term, typically 10-15% off.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical approval process for tools in this price range?
questions_asked.answer: Fatima said she has discretion up to $15K, but Sebastian handles vendor paperwork and Madison needs to sign off on integrations.

next_steps: Send contract to Sebastian for procurement review
Fatima to brief leadership after technical validation

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking in after Madison's technical review (handled separately). Fatima confirmed Madison approved the integration approach and security posture. She mentioned exec briefing got delayed due to competing priorities but she's still confident moving forward. Asked about shortening payment terms from Net 30 to Net 15.

questions_asked.question: Can we do Net 15 instead of Net 30 payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: We can accommodate Net 15 if it helps with your procurement workflow—I'll update the contract.

next_steps: Fatima to brief exec team by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Fatima and Sebastian Iyer. Sebastian raised questions about contract length, auto-renewal terms, and cancellation policy. Fatima still hasn't completed exec briefing but wants to move forward. Discussion around whether to lock in annual or explore multi-year. Sebastian cautious about committing without broader leadership visibility.

questions_asked.question: What's your cancellation policy if this doesn't deliver ROI?
questions_asked.answer: 30-day notice required, but we're confident in value delivery—happy to include success metrics in the SOW.

questions_asked.question: Sebastian, what would make you comfortable signing annual vs multi-year?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian prefers annual initially given Fatima is new to the role and wants to validate impact before longer commitment.

objections: Would like exec sponsor alignment before finalizing, especially given Fireflies is working adequately
next_steps: Schedule pricing alignment call if multi-year is considered

touches.ai_summary: Pricing and terms call with Fatima and Sebastian. Fatima still working on exec briefing but pushing to close before quarter end. Sebastian asked about discount for faster payment (Net 10) or volume discount if they add seats mid-year. Discussed 5% discount for upfront annual payment vs monthly. Fatima concerned about getting approval without exec sponsor but willing to proceed on her authority. Tone more cautious as timeline pressure increases.

questions_asked.question: Can you offer a discount for upfront annual payment?
questions_asked.answer: We can do 5% off for upfront payment—brings total to $11,685 for the year.

questions_asked.question: What if we want to add seats in Q3—can we get the same per-seat rate?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, seat additions will be pro-rated at the same $820 per seat rate through your renewal date.

questions_asked.question: Where do things stand with exec alignment?
questions_asked.answer: Fatima admitted her VP has been hard to pin down but she's confident this falls within her purview and doesn't want to delay further.

objections: Sebastian remains hesitant to sign without VP visibility given budget size and Fatima's tenure
next_steps: Fatima to make final call on annual vs monthly
Sebastian to review updated contract with 5% discount
Target signature by end of week
